title: Bacterial SNPs in the human gut microbiome associate with host BMI
method: computational; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) framework; Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v; Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk; Phenome-wide association study (PheWAS) of 12.7 mi; Systematic identification of microbial genomic str; Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) analyzing
findings: Discovered 1,358 statistically significant associations between bacterial SNPs and host BMI (Bonferroni-corrected P < 0.05), distilling to 40 independent associations after clumping | BMI-associated SNPs found in 27 bacterial species, with 12 showing no association by standard relative abundance analysis | 17 associations replicated in a geographically independent cohort | Most associations were unexplained by diet, medications, or physical exercise | Revealed BMI association of SNP in inflammatory pathway of Bilophila wadsworthia and SNPs in energy metabolism functions in Faecalibacterium prausnitzii
limitations: Only examined associations with BMI as primary phenotype | Correlation threshold of 0.3 for clumping may affect independence of findings | Covariates limited to age, sex, and species relative abundance

title: Chechi 等 - 2026 - A gut microbiome-kidney-heart axis predictive of future cardiovascular diseases_副本
method: hybrid; Multi-center observational cohort study investigat; Shotgun metagenomic sequencing combined with compu; Meta-analysis of genetic associations between huma; Cross-sectional study comparing gut microbiome (16; Multi-omics study combining metagenomics, metabolo
findings: Gut microbiome-kidney-heart axis predicts future cardiovascular diseases | Multi-omics integration reveals interconnected pathways between gut microbiota, kidney function, and cardiovascular outcomes | Microbiome-based predictive models for cardiovascular disease risk stratification
limitations: Causality cannot be established from observational data | Potential confounding by diet and medication use affecting microbiome composition | Generalizability across different populations and ethnicities requires further validation

title: Reference-free Structural Variant Detection in Microbiomes via Long-read Coassembly Graphs
method: computational; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) framework; Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v; Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk; Phenome-wide association study (PheWAS) of 12.7 mi; Systematic identification of microbial genomic str; Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) analyzing
findings: rhea outperforms existing methods for SV and horizontal gene transfer (HGT) detection | The method remains effective even when simulated reads diverge from reference genomes | Increased strain diversity does not compromise detection accuracy | Specific sequence alterations detected across environmental and fermented food microbiomes
limitations: Limited details on sample size and sequencing specifics | Generalizability to other microbiome types not fully explored

title: Mapping the gut microbial structural variations in healthy aging within the Chinese population
method: hybrid; Multi-center observational cohort study investigat; Shotgun metagenomic sequencing combined with compu; Meta-analysis of genetic associations between huma; Cross-sectional study comparing gut microbiome (16; Multi-omics study combining metagenomics, metabolo
findings: 10,367 microbial structural variations (SVs) in 60 gut microbial species were identified from Chinese populations | There is a trend of copy number loss in age-related SVs during the human aging process, with 35 core SVs consistently detected | Eight specific microbial SVs can distinguish healthy from unhealthy aging, independent of age | A 3-kbp deletion SV of Bifidobacterium pseudocatenulatum, encoding plant polysaccharide degradation, is regulated by plant-based diet and contributes to healthy aging through bile acid metabolism | Microbial SVs harboring genes in methionine-homocysteine metabolism pathway are linked to chronic kidney disease (CKD)
limitations: Study focused exclusively on Chinese population, limiting generalizability to other ethnic groups | Cross-sectional design with limited longitudinal follow-up (only 615 participants followed up for 3 years) | Functional validation of identified SVs and their mechanistic links to healthy aging was not performed

method: computational; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) framework; Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v; Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk; Phenome-wide association study (PheWAS) of 12.7 mi; Systematic identification of microbial genomic str; Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) analyzing
findings: Geography explains more microbiome variation than skin type in the human skin microbiome | Host genetics contributes previously uncharacterized structure to the skin microbiome, exemplified by distinct profiles in Chinese individuals | Genome-wide association analysis identified 107 SNPs linked to 22 microbial taxa, including phages and plasmids, implicating host genes in skin structure, immunity, and lipid metabolism | Cutibacterium acnes and its phages exhibit geographic divergence and phage-host co-adaptation | Host-infecting viruses, particularly papillomaviruses, are associated with elevated microbial diversity and immune-modulatory functions
limitations: Host DNA contamination in skin samples makes shotgun metagenomics challenging | Sample size limited to 327 individuals across 5 countries may limit generalizability | Distinguishing viruses and plasmids from host genomes remains challenging with current classifiers

title: Microbiome-wide PheWAS links gut microbial SNVs to human health and exposures
method: computational; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) framework; Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v; Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk; Phenome-wide association study (PheWAS) of 12.7 mi; Systematic identification of microbial genomic str; Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) analyzing
findings: Identified 37,945 associations with 237 host phenotypes and environmental exposures, including 3,018 associations supported by at least two cohorts for 105 host factors | Found 718 age-associated gut microbial SNVs across multiple cohorts, supporting variation in the gut microbiome as a hallmark of aging | Discovered 25 associations between microbial SNVs and diet in both Dutch and Tanzanian cohorts | Identified SNVs in an α-Melanocyte-stimulating hormone-mimicking clpB gene of Alistipes ihumii associated with host body weight and blood lipid levels | Found that SNVs of the isoleucyl-tRNA synthetase gene of Clostridium phoceensis mediate the beneficial impact of diet quality on host health
limitations: Limited population diversity despite inclusion of African cohort | Cross-sectional nature limits causal inference | Batch effects possible across cohorts

title: Host genetic regulation of human gut microbial structural variation
method: hybrid; Multi-center observational cohort study investigat; Shotgun metagenomic sequencing combined with compu; Meta-analysis of genetic associations between huma; Cross-sectional study comparing gut microbiome (16; Multi-omics study combining metagenomics, metabolo
findings: Host genetics significantly regulates gut microbial structural variation through genome-wide associations between human genetic variants and bacterial metagenome variations | ABO and FUT2 genotypes jointly determine presence of a structural variation segment in Faecalibacterium prausnitzii containing an N-acetylgalactosamine (GalNAc) utilization gene cluster | GalNAc can be used as sole carbohydrate source by F. prausnitzii strains carrying the GalNAc-metabolizing pathway, with functional validation in vitro | Other ABO-associated species including Collinsella aerofaciens can also utilize GalNAc | GalNAc utilization genes are associated with host cardiometabolic health, particularly in individuals with mucosal A-antigen
limitations: Population-specific: discovery in Dutch cohorts with limited replication in Tanzanian cohort | Cross-sectional design limits causal inference for host-microbiome interactions | Mechanistic details of how ABO/FUT2 genotypes shape intestinal environment remain to be fully elucidated

title: Structural variation in the gut microbiome associates with host health
method: computational; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) framework; Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v; Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk; Phenome-wide association study (PheWAS) of 12.7 mi; Systematic identification of microbial genomic str; Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) analyzing
findings: Structural variants (SVs) are highly prevalent in the human gut microbiome across bacterial phyla and replicate across independent cohorts (76% of SVs replicated in Dutch cohort) | SVs are enriched for CRISPR-associated and antibiotic-producing functions but depleted from housekeeping genes, suggesting a role in microbial adaptation | 124 significant associations were found between SVs and host disease risk factors, with 40 associations replicating in the independent Dutch cohort | A genomic region in Anaerostipes hadrus encoding a composite inositol catabolism-butyrate biosynthesis pathway was identified, with presence associated with lower host metabolic disease risk | SVs represent a nascent layer of variability in the microbiome associated with microbial adaptation and host health
limitations: Reference database dependency: reliance on 3,953 bacterial genomes may miss novel SVs from uncharacterized species | Algorithm limitation: ICRA may have reduced sensitivity for very rare or highly divergent strains | Population specificity: significant SV-disease associations may be specific to particular populations and not universally generalizable

title: Gut microbial structural variation associates with immune checkpoint inhibitor response
method: computational; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) framework; Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v; Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk; Phenome-wide association study (PheWAS) of 12.7 mi; Systematic identification of microbial genomic str; Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) analyzing
findings: Microbial genomic structural variants (SVs) in gut bacteria are associated with immune checkpoint inhibitor (ICI) treatment response | Associated SVs are located in multiple species including Akkermansia muciniphila, Dorea formicigenerans, and Bacteroides caccae | Genes encoding enzymes involved in glucose metabolism are harbored in SV regions associated with ICI response | This work identifies a nascent layer of gut microbiome heterogeneity that correlates with host prognosis following ICI treatment
limitations: Full limitations section not visible in truncated text | Potential confounding factors from different cancer types across cohorts | Causality cannot be established from association study alone

title: Rural–urban environmental differences shape gut microbiome and metabolome signatures linked to cardiovascular disease risk in sub-Saharan Africa
method: hybrid; Multi-center observational cohort study investigat; Shotgun metagenomic sequencing combined with compu; Meta-analysis of genetic associations between huma; Cross-sectional study comparing gut microbiome (16; Multi-omics study combining metagenomics, metabolo
findings: Rural individuals had significantly higher gut microbial alpha diversity and distinct beta diversity compared to urban residents (Shannon index p = 9.17 x 10^-12; Bray-Curtis PERMANOVA p < 0.001) | 250 out of 1008 microbial taxa differed in abundance between settings, with Succinivibrio and Desulfovibrio urban-enriched and Agathobacter and Faecalibacterium rural-enriched | Metabolomic profiling revealed distinct setting-specific signatures with prenol lipids, fatty acyls, and carboxylic acids predominant among differentially abundant metabolites | Vitamin K metabolism was identified as a major biological pathway enriched by differentially abundant metabolites | Several taxa such as Succinivibrio and Erysipelatoclostridiaceae mediated the association between urban living and elevated blood pressure
limitations: Cross-sectional design cannot establish causality between microbiome changes and CVD risk | Study conducted within a single country (Uganda) limiting generalizability to other sub-Saharan African populations | Specific bioinformatics pipeline details, software versions, and database versions not reported

title: Characterization of gut microbial structural variations as determinants of human bile acid metabolism
method: computational; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) framework; Development of rhea, a reference-free structural v; Ultra-low coverage human genome imputation from sk; Phenome-wide association study (PheWAS) of 12.7 mi; Systematic identification of microbial genomic str; Metagenome-wide association study analyzing gut mi; Metagenome-wide association study (MWAS) analyzing
findings: Characterized 8,282 structural variants (SVs) across 55 bacterial species in gut microbiomes of 1,437 individuals | Identified 809 replicable associations between bacterial SVs and bile acid parameters through metagenome-wide association analysis | Both SV-based continuous genetic makeup and discrete clusters showed correlations with bile acid metabolism | SV regulators mediate the effects of lifestyle factors on bile acid metabolism | SV-based metagenome analysis enables discovery of previously unknown bile acid transformation genes
limitations: Observational study design cannot establish causality between microbial SVs and bile acid metabolism | A considerable proportion of BA-related genes are still unknown and not captured by current analysis | Study limited to fasting plasma samples; postprandial BA dynamics not assessed

title: Dissecting multi-gene virulence phenotypes with base editing requires consideration of target-independent mutagenesis
method: experimental; CRISPR base editing was employed using temperature; High-throughput screening of a genome-wide single-
findings: Multiplexed base editing can reproducibly achieve simultaneous editing of three target genes and can be iteratively extended to produce complex multi-gene knockouts in Salmonella Typhimurium | A base-edited multi-knockout strain showed reduced virulence in vivo similar to a traditionally constructed recombineering mutant | Off-target cytidine deaminase activity was detected via whole-genome sequencing, producing variable virulence phenotypes in vivo in a clone-dependent manner | Statistical power analysis can predict how many edited mutants are needed to confidently measure fitness functions accounting for off-target mutations | Target-independent mutagenesis confounds accurate discovery of genetic interactions in bacterial pathogens
limitations: Target-independent cytidine deaminase activity produces off-target mutations in bacterial chromosomes | Variable levels of off-target editing lead to clone-dependent virulence phenotypes | Off-target mutations may confound accurate discovery of genetic interactions

title: SCLS-2025-0585-online 1..14
method: hybrid; Multi-center observational cohort study investigat; Shotgun metagenomic sequencing combined with compu; Meta-analysis of genetic associations between huma; Cross-sectional study comparing gut microbiome (16; Multi-omics study combining metagenomics, metabolo
findings: Phylogenetic differences in 85% of species were associated with exploratory behavior (FDR<0.05) | 146 million microbial SNVs were associated with 953 plasma metabolites, identifying 34 study-wide significant associations (P<2.9×10^-8) | 4-Anisic acid and D-galacturonate were identified as key metabolites linking microbial SNVs to host cognitive exploration via structural regulation of membrane transporters | 3,548 novel species-level genomes were identified compared to existing sheep databases, from a total of 5,253 species-level MAGs generated | Microbial genetic variation plays a critical role in modulating host neurocognition through metabolite regulation
limitations: Study limited to Merino sheep breed with controlled conditions; findings may not generalize to other species or human populations | Specific sequencing depth and bioinformatics pipeline details not fully reported | Causal mechanisms of SNV-driven metabolite regulation on neurocognition require further experimental validation

title: Bai 等 - 2025 - Microbial genetic composition regulates host social behavior_副本
method: experimental; CRISPR base editing was employed using temperature; High-throughput screening of a genome-wide single-
findings: Screened 3,983 E. coli knockout strains and identified 370 mutant strains that significantly reduced social aggregation behavior in C. elegans | Five mutations in the L-tyrosine biosynthesis pathway significantly alter social aggregation behavior of C. elegans via the TGF-β signaling pathway | Tyrosine was significantly enriched among differentially abundant metabolites associated with reduced social behavior | Decreased tyrosine synthesis in E. coli was identified as crucial for reduced worm aggregation behavior through GO and KEGG pathway analyses
limitations: Model organism limitations: C. elegans is a simplified model that may not fully replicate complex mammalian gut-brain interactions | Single bacterial species: Study used only E. coli mutants, whereas natural gut microbiota comprises complex communities | Mechanistic details not fully elucidated: TGF-β pathway involvement identified but detailed molecular mechanisms require further investigation
